meaning: n.不腐败,清廉,不能收买,高尚
examples: Where power has been exercised over generations, a kind of hereditary incorruptibility grows up.
A series of minor scandals has tarnished the Social Democrats' reputation for integrity and incorruptibility.
Hence incorruptibility; hence the miscarriage of unhealthy lusts; hence eyes heroically lowered before temptations.
Kim, like Lee's supporters, hope the party will benefit from his reputation for incorruptibility.
Elected Mayor of Pasto in 1995, Mr. Navarro earned a reputation for action and incorruptibility, both unusual traits among Colombian politicians.
